message.content:
Read `./.claude/progress.txt`, pick the most important pending tasks, execute it, update the file, commit, and stop.

'./.claude/progress.txt' is:
- your persistent memory.
- Important errors must be registered in order to avoid expending tokens again.
- Never run tasks not registered in it.
- Register new tasks if needed, respecting the current structure.


WORKFLOW:
0. Understand Project Facts from CLAUDE.md
1. Read ./.claude/progress.txt and identify the most important pending task;
   - Specific task prioritization is indicated/tagged/prefixed with '[P'+number+']'
   - Tasks tagged with '[P0]' must be done before anything else.
   - Tasks tagged with P bigger than 999 (e.g. '[P1000]'), are low-priority 
   tasks, they should be done when possible, but they must be done 
   (they are not ignorable); If the task takes longer and there are other 
   non-low-priority tasks, document progress and revert the status to "- [ ]".
   
2. If NO pending tasks exist, signal completion (see OUTPUT STATUS below) and stop
3. If there are tasks, mark it as IN_PROGRESS and then sequentially run:
   a. Evaluate scope: if the task involves more than ~3 files or multiple concerns,
      break it into smaller sub-tasks in progress.txt BEFORE starting. Each sub-task
      must be completable in a single iteration (small, atomic, committable change).
   b. Execute task (respecting current project structure and ./.claude/STYLE_GUIDE.md)
   c. Launch 1 review agents to deeply review the work, giving
      the entire context of the task, simulating pull request.
   d. Review output and consider improvements
   e. Implement corrections if needed
   f. Update progress.txt marking task as completed
   g. Commit task changes (not include unrelated code)
   h. IF THE COMMIT FAILS (pre-commit hook / lint error):
      - Add a [P0] priority task to progress.txt describing the exact errors
      - Retry the commit with `git commit --no-verify` to save your work
      - The NEXT iteration will fix the lint errors as its top priority
      - Do NOT spend time fixing lint in this iteration — save tokens for the next one
   i. Output status block (see below)
   j. Stop

- Nenhuma tarefa deve ser considerada completa sem testar com Playwright MVP 
  e tirar screenshots para visualização e visualizar.
  
OUTPUT STATUS (REQUIRED at end of EVERY response):
You MUST output this exact format at the END of your response:

RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": <true if ALL tasks done, false otherwise>, "task": "<completed task name>", "remaining": <number of pending tasks> }

Examples:
- Task completed but more remain: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": false, "task": "Create usePoints hook", "remaining": 5 }
- ALL tasks completed: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": true, "task": "Delete Redux files", "remaining": 0 }

CRITICAL (THINK BEFORE ACTING):
1. The FIRST thing you MUST do is write your understanding of each WORKFLOW statement
2. The SECOND thing you MUST do is write your understanding of the work in progress described in progress.txt
3. Only AFTER completing steps 1 and 2, proceed with the task execution
4. If more work is necessary, add a new task to the file, set "exit": false, and Stop
5. Only set "exit": true when ALL tasks in progress.txt are completed
6. For @semparar/* packages, read the real js code because some types are not updated correctly.
7. Never remove tasks, unless to update status;
8. Never include workarounds and add tasks to fix all you found ('as any' for example is a workaround)

- [DONE] SP_0XX0.5 - Setup Claude Code Playwright MCP.

# SP_0001.0 — Setup Storybook
- [DONE] Configure Storybook so this MFE can render screens independently of the
SuperApp.
  > Storybook 8.6.17 with @storybook/react-native-web-vite running on port 6006.
  > Custom Vite plugins: jsxInJsPlugin, dtsStubPlugin, circularFixPlugin, cjsToEsmPlugin.
  > ThemeProvider decorator in preview.tsx. Zustand store mocked in .storybook/mocks/store.ts.
  > SmokeTest story renders Text, Button, Box from DS successfully.

Context:
- This is a React Native MFE (package.json: react 18.2.0, react-native 0.73.6)
- The design system (@semparar/design-system-app ^3.39.15) already has Storybook
  configured — its setup can be used as reference if the executing agent needs it
- Babel aliases: ~ → ./src/ (babel.config.js line 27)
- TS alias: ~/store → ./@types/store.d.ts (tsconfig.json line 11) — this is a
  module augmentation for the SuperApp's Zustand store, needs mocking
- DS components depend on design tokens — may need a ThemeProvider or similar

Deliverable: `npm run storybook` opens a browser with a smoke test story that
renders at least one DS component (e.g., Button or Text from @semparar/design-system-app).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0001.1 — Validate DS components in Storybook
- [DONE] Prove that the design system components actually used in voucher screens
      render correctly in the Storybook web context.
  > 8/10 DS components render in Storybook web context.
  > BackDropBottom (Modalize) and Loading (Lottie) require native modules — cannot render on web.
  > SDUI adapters should provide web-safe fallbacks for these two.
  > Full validation story at src/__stories__/DSComponentValidation.stories.tsx.

Context:
- The voucher screens use these DS components: Text, Button, Box, InputBox,
  Alert, Icon, ListTextIcon, SeparatorHorizontal, BackDropBottom, Loading
- See existing usage in:
  - src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/view.tsx (form with inputs, balance, button)
  - src/screens/InvoiceCashbackSummary/view.tsx (review, success, error states)
  - src/screens/InvoiceCashbackOnboarding/index.tsx (list items with icons)
- Some components may not render in web — document which ones work and which don't

Deliverable: A story page rendering each DS component listed above. Annotate any
that fail or need workarounds.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0002.0 — SDUI Core: Types & Interfaces
- [DONE] Define the TypeScript type system for the SDUI architecture described in
      .claude/rfc-conteudo-dinamico.txt (sections 3.1–3.5).
  > Types at src/services/sdui/models/index.ts (180 lines).
  > Covers: SDUIScreen, SDUIComponent, SDUIContext, SDUIRichText, SDUIRule,
  >   SDUIConditional, SDUIValidation, SDUIComponentRegistry, SDUIComponentRenderer.
  > SDUI_KNOWN_TYPES const array with all 13 voucher-relevant component types.

Context:
- Screen structure: { id, context, header, body[], footer } (RFC section 3.1)
- Components: { type, props } (RFC section 3.2)
- Rich text: { type: "rich_text", children: [nodes] } (RFC section 3.3)
- Context interpolation: {{var}} pattern (RFC section 3.4)
- Conditionals: JSON Logic subset (RFC section 3.5)
- Follow project conventions: .claude/STYLE_GUIDE.md (services architecture,
  models in Models/ folders)

Deliverable: Type definitions in src/services/sdui/models/ that contract the
entire SDUI system (Screen, Component, Context, RichText, ComponentRegistry).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0002.1 — SDUI Core: Interpolation Parser
- [DONE] Implement the {{var}} replacement engine from RFC section 3.4.
  > src/services/sdui/utils/interpolation.ts — 3 functions:
  >   resolveContextPath (nested path + array index support)
  >   interpolateString ({{var}} regex replacement)
  >   interpolateDeep (recursive object/array walk)
  > 24 unit tests passing (interpolation.test.ts).
  > Storybook demo at src/__stories__/SDUIInterpolation.stories.tsx.
  > Screenshot validated via Playwright.

Context:
- Must resolve nested paths: {{product.name}}, {{balance.pointsFormatted}}
- Must deep-walk an object interpolating all string values in props
- Missing values should resolve gracefully (empty string, not crash)
- Existing interpolation example in RFC:
  "Voucher para {{product.name}}" + context.product.name = "Abastecimento"
  → "Voucher para Abastecimento"
- Types from SP_0002.0 should be used

Deliverable: Interpolation utility with unit tests.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0002.2 — SDUI Core: Component Registry & Screen Renderer
- [DONE] Build the rendering engine that turns a JSON screen definition into React
      components. This is the core of the SDUI architecture (RFC sections 3.1–3.2).
  > Registry at src/services/sdui/registry/index.ts — createRegistry, registerComponent,
  >   getRenderer, registerToDefault (immutable API + mutable default singleton).
  > ScreenRenderer at src/services/sdui/renderer/ScreenRenderer.tsx — renders header,
  >   body[], footer from SDUIScreen JSON. Applies interpolateDeep to all props.
  > FallbackComponent at src/services/sdui/renderer/FallbackComponent.tsx — visible
  >   yellow box for unknown component types (uses RN primitives for ThemeProvider safety).
  > 14 unit tests passing (registry.test.ts + ScreenRenderer.test.tsx).
  > Storybook demo at src/__stories__/SDUIScreenRenderer.stories.tsx.
  > Screenshot validated via Playwright.

Context:
- Registry maps string type → React component (RFC section 3.2 "Componentes")
- Renderer receives SDUIScreen JSON, iterates body[], renders each via registry
- Must apply interpolation (SP_0002.1) to all string props using screen context
- Unknown types should render a visible fallback (not crash)
- Types from SP_0002.0, interpolation from SP_0002.1

Deliverable: A <ScreenRenderer screen={json} /> component that renders a
screen definition from JSON.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0002.3 — SDUI Component Adapters
- [DONE] Create adapters that bridge SDUI component types to actual DS components.
  > 11 adapters implemented: heading, text, section_heading, balance_display,
  >   points_input, button, link, alert, list_text_icon, separator, icon_text_row.
  > All registered via registerAllAdapters() in src/services/sdui/adapters/index.ts.
  > 13 unit tests passing (adapters.test.tsx).
  > Storybook story at src/__stories__/SDUIAdapters.stories.tsx.
  > Screenshots validated: sdui-adapters.png + sdui-adapters-full.png.
  > Review notes: stateful PointsInputAdapter + stubbed onPress handlers are
  >   acceptable tech debt for spike phase.

Context:
- Each adapter maps SDUI props to @semparar/design-system-app component props
- Required adapters based on voucher screen analysis (TASK.pdf + existing screens):

  | SDUI type        | DS component(s)           | Used in                    |
  |------------------|---------------------------|----------------------------|
  | heading          | Text (headingMDL)         | Form title, Review title   |
  | text             | Text (configurable)       | Labels, descriptions       |
  | balance_display  | Box + Text                | "Pontos disponíveis: X"   |
  | points_input     | InputBox                  | Points/reais fields        |
  | button           | Button                    | CTA buttons                |
  | link             | Text (pressable)          | "Exibir regras de uso"     |
  | alert            | Alert                     | Info boxes in summary      |
  | list_text_icon   | ListTextIcon              | Onboarding items, rules    |
  | separator        | SeparatorHorizontal       | Between fields             |
  | icon_text_row    | Box + Icon + Text         | Regras de uso bullet items |
  | section_heading  | Text (subtitleXXL)        | Regras section titles      |

- Register all in the default registry from SP_0002.2
- DS component validation from SP_0001.1 may affect which adapters are viable

Deliverable: All voucher-relevant adapters registered in the component registry.

  > Files at src/services/sdui/mocks/ — voucherForm.ts, voucherSummary.ts, voucherConclusion.ts, voucherRules.ts.
  > Barrel export at src/services/sdui/mocks/index.ts.
  > All payloads use {{var}} interpolation for dynamic content (balance, conversion, product name).
  > Storybook validation story at src/__stories__/SDUIMockPayloads.stories.tsx.
  > Screenshot validated via Playwright (sdui-mockpayloads-full.png).
  > Review notes: icon_text_row with empty text in conclusion screen is a known spike limitation
  >   (would need a new `status_icon` type for proper centered icon rendering).

Context:

  1. Voucher form (points/reais input) — reference: InvoiceCashbackPoints/view.tsx
  2. Voucher summary (review before confirm) — reference: InvoiceCashbackSummary/view.tsx lines 47-151
  3. Voucher conclusion (success) — reference: InvoiceCashbackSummary/view.tsx lines 154-203
  4. Regras de uso (rules) — reference: TASK.pdf page 4 screenshot

  - Cashback: "Confirmar desconto" / Voucher: "Criar voucher"

- Include at least 2 product variants (Abastecimento + Estacionamento) to prove

# SP_0002.5 — Demo Stories: SDUI Rendering
- [DONE] Final demonstration — render all voucher screens from JSON through the SDUI
      engine in Storybook.
  > 5 stories in src/__stories__/SDUIDemoVoucher.stories.tsx:

  >   FullFlow (combined: product toggle + step navigation across all 4 screens).

Now let me understand the workflow and current state.

## WORKFLOW Understanding

1. Read progress.txt and find highest priority pending task
2. If no pending tasks, signal exit
3. Mark task IN_PROGRESS, then: evaluate scope → execute → review → correct → update → commit → output status → stop
4. Tasks are prioritized by `[P<n>]` tags. P0 first, then P1, etc. P1000+ are low-priority.
5. Validate with Playwright screenshots before marking done
6. If commit fails, add P0 task and commit with --no-verify

## Current State Analysis

Looking at pending tasks by priority:
- **[P1] SP989887GV** — Review RFC and add missing tasks (JSON-logic etc.)
- **[P99] SP9999999999** — Multiple tasks (lint, TASK.pdf, handlers)
- **[P1000] SP_1BGXJ78** — Autocomplete in JSON Editor (low priority)
- **No priority tag**: SP_0004.0–SP_0004.8, SP_0005.0–SP_0005.3, SP0XM01, SP0BJM07, SP_XH09Xa00, SPXH988712, SP008767GX

1→
     2→Proposta: Server Driven UI (SDUI): Um Padrão para Telas Parametrizáveis
     3→Status: Rascunho
     4→Versão: 1.0
     5→
     6→
     7→                              Resumo
     8→
     9→   Esta proposta descreve uma forma de estruturar o conteúdo das
    10→   telas parametrizáveis (vouchers, benefícios, resgates, etc)
    11→   de forma padronizada para reduzir a necessidade de deploy quando
    12→   há mudanças de texto, formatação ou ordem de elementos.
    13→
    14→   A proposta é baseada em padrões utilizados por empresas como
    15→   Airbnb, Nubank e DoorDash, adaptados para nossa realidade.
    16→
    17→
    18→Índice
    19→
    20→   1. Contexto
    21→   2. Proposta
    22→   3. Como Funciona
    23→      3.1. Estrutura de Tela
    24→      3.2. Componentes
    25→      3.3. Conteúdo Rico
    26→      3.4. Valores e Contexto
    27→      3.5. Regras e Condicionais
    28→   4. Exemplo: Tela de Voucher
    29→   5. Implementação
    30→   6. Referências
    31→
    32→
    33→1. Contexto
    34→
    35→   Muitas aplicações possuem telas parametrizáveis, funcionando como 
    36→   templates, que recebem dados do backend, definidos conforme a 
    37→   necessidade de cada template. Na prática, os valores são dinâmicos,
    38→   mas a estrutura não. A cada nova necessidade de alteração simples de
    39→   layout e formatação, como destacar um texto em negrito, um novo campo é
    40→   adicionado no JSON de resposta do servidor e uma nova regra condicional
    41→   (if) é adicionada no frontend, resultando em armazenamento de dados legados
    42→   e no acúmulo de código morto.
    43→
    44→2. Proposta: Padrão SDUI.
    45→    
    46→    É um padrão arquitetural no qual o backend define a lógica de exibição 
    47→    dos dados, indo além da definição de parâmetros textuais.
    48→
    49→    Empresas que usam esse padrão:
    50→
    51→        - Nubank: 70% das novas telas usam esse formato
    52→        - Airbnb: Busca, listagens e checkout
    53→        - DoorDash: Reduziu tempo de mudanças de semanas para horas
    54→
    55→3. Como Funciona
    56→
    57→3.1. Estrutura de Tela
    58→
    59→     Uma tela é composta por:
    60→
    61→     {
    62→       "screen": {
    63→         "id": "voucher_form",
    64→         "context": { ... },      // dados para usar nos componentes
    65→         "header": { ... },       // cabeçalho
    66→         "body": [ ... ],         // array de componentes
    67→         "footer": { ... }        // rodapé (botão de ação)
    68→       }
    69→     }
    70→
    71→     O body é um array. A ordem do array é a ordem na tela.
    72→
    73→3.2. Componentes
    74→
    75→     Cada componente tem um tipo e propriedades:
    76→
    77→     { "type": "heading", "props": { "text": "Título" } }
    78→     { "type": "input", "props": { "label": "Valor em pontos" } }
    79→     { "type": "button", "props": { "label": "Continuar" } }
    80→
    81→     O app tem um registro de componentes:
    82→
    83→     const Componentes = {
    84→       'heading': HeadingComponent,
    85→       'input': InputComponent,
    86→       'button': ButtonComponent,
    87→       'balance': BalanceComponent,
    88→       // ...
    89→     };
    90→
    91→     Quando recebe o JSON, renderiza cada item do array usando
    92→     o componente correspondente ao tipo.
    93→
    94→     Tipos que já temos no Design System podem ser mapeados
    95→     diretamente. Não precisa criar componentes novos.
    96→
    97→3.3. Conteúdo Rico
    98→
    99→     Para textos que precisam de formatação inline (negrito,
   100→     links, ícones), usamos uma estrutura de "nodes":
   101→
   102→     {
   103→       "type": "rich_text",
   104→       "children": [
   105→         { "type": "text", "value": "Confira os " },
   106→         { "type": "text", "value": "termos", "bold": true },
   107→         { "type": "text", "value": " ou " },
   108→         { "type": "link", "text": "fale conosco", "to": "app://suporte" }
   109→       ]
   110→     }
   111→
   112→     Por que não markdown?
   113→
   114→     Se usarmos [texto](url) para links e depois precisarmos
   115→     adicionar tracking ou confirmação, temos que refatorar o
   116→     parser.
   117→
   118→     Com nodes, é só adicionar uma propriedade:
   119→
   120→     {
   121→       "type": "link",
   122→       "text": "fale conosco",
   123→       "to": "app://suporte",
   124→       "trackEvent": "suporte_click"    // adicionado depois
   125→     }
   126→
   127→3.4. Valores e Contexto
   128→
   129→
   130→     O Backend envia os valores no campo context:
   131→     {
   132→       "context": {
   133→         "balance": {
   134→           "points": 25720,
   135→           "value": 7716.00
   136→         }
   137→       }
   138→     }
   139→
   140→
   141→     Para textos que dependem do contexto, usamos interpolação:
   142→
   143→     { "type": "heading", "props": { "text": "Voucher para {{product.name}}" } }
   144→
   145→     O app substitui {{product.name}} pelo valor do contexto.
   146→
   147→3.5. Regras e Condicionais
   148→
   149→     Para controlar visibilidade e habilitação, usamos regras
   150→     simples baseadas em JSON Logic:
   151→
   152→     Botão habilitado só quando valor > 0:
   153→
   154→     {
   155→       "type": "button",
   156→       "props": {
   157→         "label": "Continuar",
   158→         "enabled": {
   159→           "rule": { ">": [{ "var": "form.points" }, 0] }
   160→         }
   161→       }
   162→     }
   163→
   164→     Os operadores suportados são básicos:
   165→     - var (acesso a dados)
   166→     - ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparação)
   167→     - and, or, ! (lógica)
   168→
   169→     Não tem loops, não tem eval, não tem risco.
   170→
   171→     Validação de inputs segue o mesmo padrão:
   172→
   173→     {
   174→       "type": "input",
   175→       "props": {
   176→         "id": "points",
   177→         "validation": {
   178→           "rules": [
   179→             {
   180→               "rule": { ">": [{ "var": "value" }, 0] },
   181→               "message": "Informe um valor maior que zero"
   182→             }
   183→           ]
   184→         }
   185→       }
   186→     }
   187→
   188→5. Exemplo: Tela de Voucher
   189→
   190→   Estrutura completa para a tela de voucher:
   191→
   192→   {
   193→     "screen": {
   194→       "id": "voucher_form",
   195→       "context": {
   196→         "product": {
   197→           "key": "abastecimento",
   198→           "name": "Abastecimento"
   199→         },
   200→         "balance": {
   201→           "points": 25720,
   202→           "pointsFormatted": "25.720",
   203→           "valueFormatted": "R$ 7.716,00"
   204→         }
   205→       },
   206→       "header": {
   207→         "type": "nav_header",
   208→         "props": {
   209→           "title": "Voucher para {{product.name}}",
   210→           "showBack": true
   211→         }
   212→       },
   213→       "body": [
   214→         {
   215→           "type": "heading",
   216→           "props": { "text": "Defina o desconto para seu {{product.key}}" }
   217→         },
   218→         {
   219→           "type": "balance_display",
   220→           "props": {
   221→             "label": "Pontos disponíveis",
   222→             "points": "{{balance.pointsFormatted}}",
   223→             "value": "{{balance.valueFormatted}}"
   224→           }
   225→         },
   226→         {
   227→           "type": "points_input",
   228→           "props": {
   229→             "id": "points",
   230→             "label": "Valor em pontos",
   231→             "type": "numeric"
   232→           }
   233→         },
   234→         {
   235→           "type": "points_input",
   236→           "props": {
   237→             "id": "reais",
   238→             "label": "Valor em reais",
   239→             "type": "currency"
   240→           }
   241→         },
   242→         {
   243→           "type": "link",
   244→           "props": {
   245→             "text": "Exibir regras de uso",
   246→             "to": "app://voucher/rules"
   247→           }
   248→         }
   249→       ],
   250→       "footer": {
   251→         "type": "button",
   252→         "props": {
   253→           "label": "Continuar",
   254→           "style": "primary",
   255→           "enabled": {
   256→             "rule": { ">": [{ "var": "form.points" }, 0] }
   257→           }
   258→         }
   259→       }
   260→     }
   261→   }
   262→
   263→   O mesmo código renderiza Abastecimento, Estacionamento,
   264→   Seguro Auto e IPVA. Mudando apenas o context, não o app.
   265→
   266→
   267→6. Implementação
   268→
   269→   6.1. O que precisa ser feito
   270→
   271→        - Registro de componentes (mapeamento tipo ‣ componente)
   272→        - Renderizador que itera o array e renderiza cada tipo
   273→        - Parser de interpolação (substituir {{var}})
   274→        - Avaliador de regras (subset de JSON Logic)
   275→
   276→   6.2. O que não muda
   277→
   278→        - Design System continua o mesmo
   279→        - Componentes existentes são reutilizados
   280→        - Telas atuais continuam funcionando
   281→        - Backend continua sendo Lambda que recebe/devolve JSON
   282→
   283→   6.3. Escopo inicial
   284→
   285→        Implementar na tela de Voucher (SPMAIS-34018) que já está
   286→        com feature toggle. É o cenário ideal:
   287→
   288→        - Feature nova, sem legado
   289→        - 4 variações do mesmo fluxo
   290→        - Retrabalho previsível (produto vai pedir ajustes)
   291→
   292→   6.4. Validação
   293→
   294→        O JSON pode ser validado antes de salvar no backend e
   295→        antes de renderizar no app. Componentes desconhecidos
   296→        usam fallback. Regras inválidas retornam false.
   297→
   298→7. Referências
   299→
   300→   [AIRBNB]    "A Deep Dive into Airbnb's Server-Driven UI System"
   301→               https://medium.com/airbnb-engineering/a-deep-dive-into-airbnbs-server-driven-ui-system-842244c5f5
   302→
   303→   [NUBANK]    "Backend Driven Content at Nubank"
   304→               https://building.nubank.com/server-driven-ui-framework-at-nubank/
   305→
   306→   [JSONLOGIC] JSON Logic - Regras em JSON
   307→               https://jsonlogic.com/
   308→

Let me create a comprehensive report: ## SDUI Implementation - Complete Analysis ### Overview The SDUI (Server Driven UI) implementation is located at `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/` and provides a framework for rendering parameterizable screens from backend JSON with dynamic interpolation, conditional rendering, and a component registry pattern. --- ## 1. TYPE SYSTEM & MODELS **File:** `/src/services/sdui/models/index.ts` (180 lines) **Exports:** - `SDUIContextValue` - Union type for context values: string | number | boolean | null | SDUIContext | SDUIContextValue[] - `SDUIContext` - Dictionary interface mapping string keys to context values - `SDUIVarOperator` - JSON Logic var operator: `{ var: 'path.to.value' }` - `SDUILogicPrimitive` - Primitive types used in rules: string | number | boolean | null - `SDUILogicOperand` - Operand type: SDUIVarOperator | SDUILogicPrimitive | SDUIRule - `SDUIComparisonRule` - Supports: `==`, `!=`, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=` - `SDUILogicalRule` - Logical operators: `and`, `or`, `!` (not) - `SDUIRule` - Union of SDUIComparisonRule | SDUILogicalRule - `SDUIConditional` - Conditional wrapper: `{ rule: SDUIRule }` - `SDUIValidationRule` - Single validation: `{ rule: SDUIRule, message: string }` - `SDUIValidation` - Multiple validations: `{ rules: SDUIValidationRule[] }` - **Rich Text Types:** - `SDUITextNode` - Text with optional bold, italic, color - `SDUILinkNode` - Link with text, to, and trackEvent - `SDUIIconNode` - Icon with name, color, size - `SDUILineBreakNode` - Line break marker - `SDUIRichText` - Container for rich text children - `SDUIPropValue` - Prop value union including all above types plus arrays and objects - `SDUIComponentProps` - Generic component props dictionary - `SDUIComponent` - Component with type, props, and optional visible conditional - `SDUIHeader`, `SDUIFooter` - Type aliases for SDUIComponent - **Screen Structure:** - `SDUIScreen` - Screen with id, context, optional header/footer, required body array - `SDUIScreenResponse` - Server response wrapper: `{ screen: SDUIScreen }` - **Registry & Rendering:** - `SDUIComponentRenderer

` - React ComponentType accepting props and context - `SDUIComponentRegistry` - Mapping of type strings to renderers - **Known Types:** - `SDUI_KNOWN_TYPES` - Constant array of pre-defined component types (13 types) - `SDUIKnownType` - Type derived from SDUI_KNOWN_TYPES --- ## 2. INTERPOLATION UTILITIES **File:** `/src/services/sdui/utils/interpolation.ts` (62 lines) **Exports:** - `resolveContextPath(context: SDUIContext, path: string): SDUIContextValue` - Resolves dot-notation paths (e.g., `product.name`, `tags.0`) - Returns empty string for null/undefined/missing paths - Supports array indexing by numeric segment - Returns empty string for non-object traversals - `interpolateString(template: string, context: SDUIContext): string` - Replaces `{{path}}` placeholders with context values - Uses regex: `/\{\{([^}]+)\}\}/g` - Returns empty string for missing paths or non-scalar values - Converts all values to strings - `interpolateDeep(value: T, context: SDUIContext): T` - Recursively interpolates entire object/array structures - Preserves non-string primitives (numbers, booleans, null) - Type-safe generic with T return type **Test Coverage:** 37 tests in `interpolation.test.ts` - Nested path resolution - Array element access - Multiple placeholders - Missing values - Type preservation - Deep object/array interpolation --- ## 3. COMPONENT REGISTRY **File:** `/src/services/sdui/registry/index.ts` (39 lines) **Exports:** - `createRegistry(initial?: SDUIComponentRegistry): SDUIComponentRegistry` - Creates new registry (immutable - returns new object) - Optionally accepts initial components - `registerComponent(registry: SDUIComponentRegistry, type: string, renderer: SDUIComponentRenderer): SDUIComponentRegistry` - Adds/overwrites component in registry (immutable) - Returns new registry object - `getRenderer(registry: SDUIComponentRegistry, type: string): SDUIComponentRenderer | undefined` - Looks up renderer by type name - Returns undefined if not found - `getDefaultRegistry(): SDUIComponentRegistry` - Returns singleton default registry (mutable state) - `registerToDefault(type: string, renderer: SDUIComponentRenderer): void` - Mutates default registry directly - Used by adapter registration system **Key Pattern:** Immutable API for creating/updating registries, but maintains one mutable default registry for bootstrap. **Test Coverage:** 8 tests in `registry.test.ts` - Immutability preservation - Component registration and lookup - Default registry mutation - Registry initialization with initial components --- ## 4. SCREEN RENDERER **File:** `/src/services/sdui/renderer/ScreenRenderer.tsx` (67 lines) **Export:** `default as ScreenRenderer` **Props Interface:** ```typescript interface ScreenRendererProps { screen: SDUIScreen; registry?: SDUIComponentRegistry; } ``` **Behavior:** - Renders three sections: header, body (flex: 1), footer - Uses default registry if none provided - For each component: 1. Interpolates props with screen context 2. Looks up renderer from registry 3. Renders with `` 4. Falls back to FallbackComponent if renderer not found - Passes original context to component (before interpolation) - Uses `${type}-${index}` as key for body items **Key Design:** Component-aware about context - both interpolated props AND original context available to adapters. **Test Coverage:** 5 tests in `ScreenRenderer.test.tsx` - Header, body, footer rendering - Interpolation application - Fallback rendering for unknown types - Optional header/footer handling - Empty body handling --- ## 5. FALLBACK COMPONENT **File:** `/src/services/sdui/renderer/FallbackComponent.tsx` (34 lines) **Export:** `default as FallbackComponent` **Props Interface:** ```typescript interface FallbackProps { props: SDUIComponentProps; type: string; } ``` **Behavior:** - Dev-only component for unregistered types - Renders yellow box with warning - Shows component type name and JSON props - Uses React Native primitives intentionally (no ThemeProvider dependency) - Useful for debugging missing adapters --- ## 6. ADAPTERS REGISTRY & EXPORTS **File:** `/src/services/sdui/adapters/index.ts` (39 lines) **Adapters Implemented (11 total):** | Type | File | Maps To | Props | |------|------|---------|-------| | `heading` | HeadingAdapter.tsx | DS Text (headingMDL) | text, color, mt, mb, align, testID | | `text` | TextAdapter.tsx | DS Text | text, type, color, colorBold, mt, mb, mr, ml, align, underline, testID | | `balance_display` | BalanceDisplayAdapter.tsx | DS Box + Text | label, pointsFormatted, priceFormatted, mt, mb | | `points_input` | PointsInputAdapter.tsx | DS InputBox | label, placeholder, keyboardType, textRight, textError, mb, contextKey | | `button` | ButtonAdapter.tsx | DS Button | text, disabled, mt, mb, icon, isLoading, testID | | `link` | LinkAdapter.tsx | DS Text (underlined) | text, color, mt, mb, align | | `alert` | AlertAdapter.tsx | DS Alert | label, type, icon, mb | | `list_text_icon` | ListTextIconAdapter.tsx | DS ListTextIcon | items[], type, ml, mr, mb | | `separator` | SeparatorAdapter.tsx | DS SeparatorHorizontal | bgColor, height, width, mt, mb | | `icon_text_row` | IconTextRowAdapter.tsx | DS Box + Icon + Text | icon, iconColor, iconSize, text, textType, textColor, mb | | `section_heading` | SectionHeadingAdapter.tsx | DS Text (subtitleXXL) | text, color, mt, mb, align | **Exports:** - `adapters` - Object mapping type strings to adapter components - `registerAllAdapters(): void` - Registers all adapters to default registry (loops through and calls `registerToDefault`) **Key Design:** - All adapters use `@semparar/design-system-app` components (FACT24WR54SL09 compliance) - Each adapter accepts `{ props: SpecificProps, context: SDUIContext }` - Context passed but not used in v1 (prepared for dynamic validation/interpolation) - Type casting workaround noted in FIXME comment (suggests future improvement to typed props) **Test Coverage:** 34 tests in `adapters.test.tsx` - All 11 adapters have basic render tests - List and composition tests - Mocked design system components --- ## 7. MOCK DATA / USAGE EXAMPLES **Files:** `/src/services/sdui/mocks/` **Exports from `index.ts`:** ```typescript export { voucherFormAbastecimento, voucherFormEstacionamento, } from './voucherForm'; export { voucherSummaryAbastecimento, voucherSummaryEstacionamento, } from './voucherSummary'; export { voucherConclusionAbastecimento, voucherConclusionEstacionamento, } from './voucherConclusion'; export { voucherRulesAbastecimento, voucherRulesEstacionamento, } from './voucherRules'; ``` **Mock Screens:** 1. **voucherForm** - Input form screen - balance_display showing available points - Two points_input fields (points, reais) - Separator and text showing conversion rate - Link to rules - Button footer 2. **voucherSummary** - Review screen - Displays selected points and reais - Text and separator elements - Alert box - Button footer 3. **voucherConclusion** - Success screen - icon_text_row with check icon - section_heading with success message - Text and link elements - Navigation button footer 4. **voucherRules** - Information screen - Multiple section_heading sections - icon_text_row items (15+ rows) - Heavy use of interpolation for dynamic values - Button footer **Context Pattern:** All mocks include context with nested structure: ```typescript context: { product: { code, key, name }, balance: { points, pointsFormatted, priceFormatted }, selectedPoints: { formatted }, selectedReais: { formatted }, conversion: { pointsQuantity, valueFormatted }, voucher: { validityDays }, support: { phone } } ``` --- ## 8. FEATURES NOT YET IMPLEMENTED **Defined in Type System but Not Evaluated:** 1. **JSON Logic Evaluation** (SDUIRule, SDUIConditional) - Types defined: `SDUIComparisonRule`, `SDUILogicalRule` - No evaluation function (e.g., `evaluateRule()`) exists - `visible: SDUIConditional` on components not processed by renderer - **Ready for implementation** - needs evaluator function 2. **Validation Rules** (SDUIValidation) - Types defined: `SDUIValidationRule`, `SDUIValidation` - No validation execution function - PointsInputAdapter has error state but no validation integration - **Ready for implementation** - needs validation executor 3. **Action Handlers / Event System** - Button and Link adapters have empty `onPress={() => {}}` - No event dispatcher or action payload system - Links have `trackEvent` property in models but not wired - **Needs design** - requires action/event protocol 4. **Rich Text Rendering** (SDUIRichText) - Types fully defined: TextNode, LinkNode, IconNode, LineBreakNode - No adapter exists - Would require special rendering logic for inline formatting - **Ready for implementation** - needs RichTextAdapter 5. **Form State Management** - PointsInputAdapter uses local useState - No integration with context or store - `contextKey` property defined but unused - **Needs integration** - should sync to context/store --- ## 9. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE ``` src/services/sdui/ ├── models/ │ └── index.ts # Type system (180 lines) ├── utils/ │ ├── interpolation.ts # Interpolation logic (62 lines) │ └── interpolation.test.ts # 37 tests ├── registry/ │ ├── index.ts # Registry functions (39 lines) │ └── registry.test.ts # 8 tests ├── renderer/ │ ├── ScreenRenderer.tsx # Main renderer (67 lines) │ ├── ScreenRenderer.test.tsx # 5 tests │ ├── FallbackComponent.tsx # Dev fallback (34 lines) │ └── index.ts # Exports ├── adapters/ │ ├── HeadingAdapter.tsx # 35 lines │ ├── TextAdapter.tsx # 56 lines │ ├── BalanceDisplayAdapter.tsx # 33 lines │ ├── PointsInputAdapter.tsx # 58 lines │ ├── ButtonAdapter.tsx # 36 lines │ ├── LinkAdapter.tsx # 35 lines │ ├── AlertAdapter.tsx # 24 lines │ ├── ListTextIconAdapter.tsx # 39 lines │ ├── SeparatorAdapter.tsx # 37 lines │ ├── IconTextRowAdapter.tsx # 42 lines │ ├── SectionHeadingAdapter.tsx # 27 lines │ ├── index.ts # 39 lines │ └── adapters.test.tsx # 34 tests └── mocks/ ├── index.ts # Re-exports (20 lines) ├── voucherForm.ts # 194 lines ├── voucherSummary.ts # 198 lines ├── voucherConclusion.ts # 136 lines └── voucherRules.ts # 374 lines ``` --- ## 10. TEST COVERAGE SUMMARY - **Total Tests:** 84 tests - Interpolation: 37 tests - Registry: 8 tests - ScreenRenderer: 5 tests - Adapters: 34 tests - **Files Tested:** 4 core modules - **Coverage Areas:** Basic functionality, edge cases, fallback behavior, immutability --- ## 11. KEY ARCHITECTURAL DECISIONS 1. **Immutable Registry API** - New registries returned on each operation, but single mutable default registry 2. **Component Receives Both Interpolated Props AND Context** - Adapters can access original context if needed 3. **Design System Dependency** - All adapters use @semparar/design-system-app (enforces consistency) 4. **Fallback Component** - Uses React Native primitives, prevents ThemeProvider crashes 5. **Deep Interpolation** - Recursive tree traversal handles nested objects and arrays 6. **Type-First Design** - Complete type system defined before implementation of evaluation logic --- ## 12. INTEGRATION POINTS **Required for Full Function:** - Rule evaluator (for conditionals and validation) - Action dispatcher (for button/link clicks) - Form state integration (for input fields) - Rich text renderer adapter - Backend API contract definition **Currently Working:** - Screen rendering - Context interpolation - Component registration - Design system integration - Fallback handling --- ## Summary The SDUI implementation is a **well-architected, type-safe foundation** for server-driven UI rendering. It currently provides: - Complete type system (13 component types) - Production-ready interpolation with 37 tests - 11 functional adapters for voucher flows - Extensible registry pattern - 4 complete mock screens for testing The system is **84% feature-complete** with JSON Logic evaluation, validation, and action handling ready for implementation based on the defined type system. The code is clean, well-tested, and follows project conventions (Design System integration, immutability patterns, comprehensive exports).

Falls back to FallbackComponent if renderer not found - Passes original context to component (before interpolation) - Uses `${type}-${index}` as key for body items **Key Design:** Component-aware about context - both interpolated props AND original context available to adapters. **Test Coverage:** 5 tests in `ScreenRenderer.test.tsx` - Header, body, footer rendering - Interpolation application - Fallback rendering for unknown types - Optional header/footer handling - Empty body handling --- ## 5. FALLBACK COMPONENT **File:** `/src/services/sdui/renderer/FallbackComponent.tsx` (34 lines) **Export:** `default as FallbackComponent` **Props Interface:** ```typescript interface FallbackProps { props: SDUIComponentProps; type: string; } ``` **Behavior:** - Dev-only component for unregistered types - Renders yellow box with warning - Shows component type name and JSON props - Uses React Native primitives intentionally (no ThemeProvider dependency) - Useful for debugging missing adapters --- ## 6. ADAPTERS REGISTRY & EXPORTS **File:** `/src/services/sdui/adapters/index.ts` (39 lines) **Adapters Implemented (11 total):** | Type | File | Maps To | Props | |------|------|---------|-------| | `heading` | HeadingAdapter.tsx | DS Text (headingMDL) | text, color, mt, mb, align, testID | | `text` | TextAdapter.tsx | DS Text | text, type, color, colorBold, mt, mb, mr, ml, align, underline, testID | | `balance_display` | BalanceDisplayAdapter.tsx | DS Box + Text | label, pointsFormatted, priceFormatted, mt, mb | | `points_input` | PointsInputAdapter.tsx | DS InputBox | label, placeholder, keyboardType, textRight, textError, mb, contextKey | | `button` | ButtonAdapter.tsx | DS Button | text, disabled, mt, mb, icon, isLoading, testID | | `link` | LinkAdapter.tsx | DS Text (underlined) | text, color, mt, mb, align | | `alert` | AlertAdapter.tsx | DS Alert | label, type, icon, mb | | `list_text_icon` | ListTextIconAdapter.tsx | DS ListTextIcon | items[], type, ml, mr, mb | | `separator` | SeparatorAdapter.tsx | DS SeparatorHorizontal | bgColor, height, width, mt, mb | | `icon_text_row` | IconTextRowAdapter.tsx | DS Box + Icon + Text | icon, iconColor, iconSize, text, textType, textColor, mb | | `section_heading` | SectionHeadingAdapter.tsx | DS Text (subtitleXXL) | text, color, mt, mb, align | **Exports:** - `adapters` - Object mapping type strings to adapter components - `registerAllAdapters(): void` - Registers all adapters to default registry (loops through and calls `registerToDefault`) **Key Design:** - All adapters use `@semparar/design-system-app` components (FACT24WR54SL09 compliance) - Each adapter accepts `{ props: SpecificProps, context: SDUIContext }` - Context passed but not used in v1 (prepared for dynamic validation/interpolation) - Type casting workaround noted in FIXME comment (suggests future improvement to typed props) **Test Coverage:** 34 tests in `adapters.test.tsx` - All 11 adapters have basic render tests - List and composition tests - Mocked design system components --- ## 7. MOCK DATA / USAGE EXAMPLES **Files:** `/src/services/sdui/mocks/` **Exports from `index.ts`:** ```typescript export { voucherFormAbastecimento, voucherFormEstacionamento, } from './voucherForm'; export { voucherSummaryAbastecimento, voucherSummaryEstacionamento, } from './voucherSummary'; export { voucherConclusionAbastecimento, voucherConclusionEstacionamento, } from './voucherConclusion'; export { voucherRulesAbastecimento, voucherRulesEstacionamento, } from './voucherRules'; ``` **Mock Screens:** 1. **voucherForm** - Input form screen - balance_display showing available points - Two points_input fields (points, reais) - Separator and text showing conversion rate - Link to rules - Button footer 2. **voucherSummary** - Review screen - Displays selected points and reais - Text and separator elements - Alert box - Button footer 3. **voucherConclusion** - Success screen - icon_text_row with check icon - section_heading with success message - Text and link elements - Navigation button footer 4. **voucherRules** - Information screen - Multiple section_heading sections - icon_text_row items (15+ rows) - Heavy use of interpolation for dynamic values - Button footer **Context Pattern:** All mocks include context with nested structure: ```typescript context: { product: { code, key, name }, balance: { points, pointsFormatted, priceFormatted }, selectedPoints: { formatted }, selectedReais: { formatted }, conversion: { pointsQuantity, valueFormatted }, voucher: { validityDays }, support: { phone } } ``` --- ## 8. FEATURES NOT YET IMPLEMENTED **Defined in Type System but Not Evaluated:** 1. **JSON Logic Evaluation** (SDUIRule, SDUIConditional) - Types defined: `SDUIComparisonRule`, `SDUILogicalRule` - No evaluation function (e.g., `evaluateRule()`) exists - `visible: SDUIConditional` on components not processed by renderer - **Ready for implementation** - needs evaluator function 2. **Validation Rules** (SDUIValidation) - Types defined: `SDUIValidationRule`, `SDUIValidation` - No validation execution function - PointsInputAdapter has error state but no validation integration - **Ready for implementation** - needs validation executor 3. **Action Handlers / Event System** - Button and Link adapters have empty `onPress={() => {}}` - No event dispatcher or action payload system - Links have `trackEvent` property in models but not wired - **Needs design** - requires action/event protocol 4. **Rich Text Rendering** (SDUIRichText) - Types fully defined: TextNode, LinkNode, IconNode, LineBreakNode - No adapter exists - Would require special rendering logic for inline formatting - **Ready for implementation** - needs RichTextAdapter 5. **Form State Management** - PointsInputAdapter uses local useState - No integration with context or store - `contextKey` property defined but unused - **Needs integration** - should sync to context/store --- ## 9. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE ``` src/services/sdui/ ├── models/ │ └── index.ts # Type system (180 lines) ├── utils/ │ ├── interpolation.ts # Interpolation logic (62 lines) │ └── interpolation.test.ts # 37 tests ├── registry/ │ ├── index.ts # Registry functions (39 lines) │ └── registry.test.ts # 8 tests ├── renderer/ │ ├── ScreenRenderer.tsx # Main renderer (67 lines) │ ├── ScreenRenderer.test.tsx # 5 tests │ ├── FallbackComponent.tsx # Dev fallback (34 lines) │ └── index.ts # Exports ├── adapters/ │ ├── HeadingAdapter.tsx # 35 lines │ ├── TextAdapter.tsx # 56 lines │ ├── BalanceDisplayAdapter.tsx # 33 lines │ ├── PointsInputAdapter.tsx # 58 lines │ ├── ButtonAdapter.tsx # 36 lines │ ├── LinkAdapter.tsx # 35 lines │ ├── AlertAdapter.tsx # 24 lines │ ├── ListTextIconAdapter.tsx # 39 lines │ ├── SeparatorAdapter.tsx # 37 lines │ ├── IconTextRowAdapter.tsx # 42 lines │ ├── SectionHeadingAdapter.tsx # 27 lines │ ├── index.ts # 39 lines │ └── adapters.test.tsx # 34 tests └── mocks/ ├── index.ts # Re-exports (20 lines) ├── voucherForm.ts # 194 lines ├── voucherSummary.ts # 198 lines ├── voucherConclusion.ts # 136 lines └── voucherRules.ts # 374 lines ``` --- ## 10. TEST COVERAGE SUMMARY - **Total Tests:** 84 tests - Interpolation: 37 tests - Registry: 8 tests - ScreenRenderer: 5 tests - Adapters: 34 tests - **Files Tested:** 4 core modules - **Coverage Areas:** Basic functionality, edge cases, fallback behavior, immutability --- ## 11. KEY ARCHITECTURAL DECISIONS 1. **Immutable Registry API** - New registries returned on each operation, but single mutable default registry 2. **Component Receives Both Interpolated Props AND Context** - Adapters can access original context if needed 3. **Design System Dependency** - All adapters use @semparar/design-system-app (enforces consistency) 4. **Fallback Component** - Uses React Native primitives, prevents ThemeProvider crashes 5. **Deep Interpolation** - Recursive tree traversal handles nested objects and arrays 6. **Type-First Design** - Complete type system defined before implementation of evaluation logic --- ## 12. INTEGRATION POINTS **Required for Full Function:** - Rule evaluator (for conditionals and validation) - Action dispatcher (for button/link clicks) - Form state integration (for input fields) - Rich text renderer adapter - Backend API contract definition **Currently Working:** - Screen rendering - Context interpolation - Component registration - Design system integration - Fallback handling --- ## Summary The SDUI implementation is a **well-architected, type-safe foundation** for server-driven UI rendering. It currently provides: - Complete type system (13 component types) - Production-ready interpolation with 37 tests - 11 functional adapters for voucher flows - Extensible registry pattern - 4 complete mock screens for testing The system is **84% feature-complete** with JSON Logic evaluation, validation, and action handling ready for implementation based on the defined type system. The code is clean, well-tested, and follows project conventions (Design System integration, immutability patterns, comprehensive exports).

| 3.5 `enabled` conditional on buttons | **NOT PROCESSED** | ButtonAdapter ignores `enabled` |

# SP_0006.0 — JSON Logic Evaluator
- [ ] Implement the JSON Logic rule evaluator described in RFC section 3.5.

Context:
- RFC defines operators: var (data access), ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparison),
  and, or, ! (logical)
- Types already exist: SDUIRule, SDUIComparisonRule, SDUILogicalRule,
  SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional (in src/services/sdui/models/index.ts)
- Needs a function: evaluateRule(rule: SDUIRule, context: SDUIContext): boolean
- var operator uses resolveContextPath from interpolation.ts
- Invalid/malformed rules should return false (RFC 6.4: "Regras inválidas
  retornam false")
- Must be pure function, no side effects
- Reference: https://jsonlogic.com/

Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts with evaluateRule() +
comprehensive unit tests covering all operators and edge cases.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0006.1 — Component Visibility & Button Enabled Conditionals
- [ ] Wire the JSON Logic evaluator into ScreenRenderer for component
      visibility and button enabled state.

Context:
- SDUIComponent already has optional `visible: SDUIConditional` field
- ScreenRenderer currently ignores `visible` — should skip rendering when
  evaluateRule returns false
- ButtonAdapter should process `enabled: { rule: SDUIRule }` in props —
  disable button when rule evaluates to false
- Depends on: SP_0006.0 (evaluateRule)
- Update existing ScreenRenderer.test.tsx and adapters.test.tsx
- Add Storybook demo showing conditional visibility toggle

Deliverable: Components with `visible` conditionals are hidden/shown based on
context. Buttons with `enabled` rules are disabled/enabled dynamically.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0006.2 — Input Validation Rules
- [ ] Implement validation rule execution for SDUI input components.

Context:
- RFC section 3.5 defines validation pattern:
  { "validation": { "rules": [{ "rule": {...}, "message": "error text" }] } }
- Types exist: SDUIValidationRule, SDUIValidation
- PointsInputAdapter has textError prop but no validation integration
- Needs: validateField(value, validation: SDUIValidation, context): string | null
  (returns first failing rule's message, or null if valid)
- Depends on: SP_0006.0 (evaluateRule)
- Should integrate with PointsInputAdapter's error display
- Add Storybook demo showing validation in action

Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/validation.ts with validateField() +
integration into PointsInputAdapter + unit tests.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0006.3 — Rich Text Adapter
- [ ] Implement the rich_text component adapter described in RFC section 3.3.

Context:
- RFC defines rich text as array of typed nodes:
  - text: { type: "text", value: string, bold?: boolean, italic?: boolean, color?: string }
  - link: { type: "link", text: string, to: string, trackEvent?: string }
  - icon: { type: "icon", name: string, color?: string, size?: number }
  - linebreak: { type: "linebreak" }
- Types already defined: SDUIRichText, SDUITextNode, SDUILinkNode, SDUIIconNode,
  SDUILineBreakNode (in models/index.ts)
- Should render inline — all nodes in a single wrapping Text component
  (React Native supports nested Text for inline formatting)
- Links should use DS Text with underline style (similar to LinkAdapter)
- Icons use DS Icon component inline
- Register as "rich_text" type in adapter registry
- Add mock payload using rich_text to test inline formatting

Deliverable: RichTextAdapter registered in SDUI registry + unit tests +
Storybook demo showing mixed bold/link/icon inline text.

implemented nor have tasks in progress.txt, review it and add new tasks.
+  > Reviewed RFC sections 3.1–3.5 against current implementation.
+  > Found 5 gaps: JSON Logic evaluator, validation executor, rich text adapter,
+  >   component visibility (visible conditional), button enabled conditional.
+  > Created tasks: SP_0006.0 (JSON Logic), SP_0006.1 (visibility + enabled),
+  >   SP_0006.2 (validation), SP_0006.3 (rich text adapter).
Context: we identified that JSON-logic and another things was not considered,
fix it.
+=== PHASE 6: RFC MISSING IMPLEMENTATIONS ======================================
+NOTE: These tasks implement RFC features that have types defined (SP_0002.0)
+but no runtime code. They complete the SDUI engine to full RFC spec.
+# SP_0006.0 — JSON Logic Evaluator
+- [ ] Implement the JSON Logic rule evaluator described in RFC section 3.5.
+Context:
+- RFC defines operators: var (data access), ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparison),
+  and, or, ! (logical)
+- Types already exist: SDUIRule, SDUIComparisonRule, SDUILogicalRule,
+  SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional (in src/services/sdui/models/index.ts)
+- Needs a function: evaluateRule(rule: SDUIRule, context: SDUIContext): boolean
+- var operator uses resolveContextPath from interpolation.ts
+- Invalid/malformed rules should return false (RFC 6.4: "Regras inválidas
+  retornam false")
+- Must be pure function, no side effects
+- Reference: https://jsonlogic.com/
+Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts with evaluateRule() +
+comprehensive unit tests covering all operators and edge cases.
+# SP_0006.1 — Component Visibility & Button Enabled Conditionals
+- [ ] Wire the JSON Logic evaluator into ScreenRenderer for component
+      visibility and button enabled state.
+Context:
+- SDUIComponent already has optional `visible: SDUIConditional` field
+- ScreenRenderer currently ignores `visible` — should skip rendering when
+  evaluateRule returns false
+- ButtonAdapter should process `enabled: { rule: SDUIRule }` in props —
+  disable button when rule evaluates to false
+- Depends on: SP_0006.0 (evaluateRule)
+- Update existing ScreenRenderer.test.tsx and adapters.test.tsx
+- Add Storybook demo showing conditional visibility toggle
+Deliverable: Components with `visible` conditionals are hidden/shown based on
+context. Buttons with `enabled` rules are disabled/enabled dynamically.
+# SP_0006.2 — Input Validation Rules
+- [ ] Implement validation rule execution for SDUI input components.
+Context:
+- RFC section 3.5 defines validation pattern:
+  { "validation": { "rules": [{ "rule": {...}, "message": "error text" }] } }
+- Types exist: SDUIValidationRule, SDUIValidation
+- PointsInputAdapter has textError prop but no validation integration
+- Needs: validateField(value, validation: SDUIValidation, context): string | null
+  (returns first failing rule's message, or null if valid)
+- Depends on: SP_0006.0 (evaluateRule)
+- Should integrate with PointsInputAdapter's error display
+- Add Storybook demo showing validation in action
+Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/validation.ts with validateField() +
+integration into PointsInputAdapter + unit tests.
+# SP_0006.3 — Rich Text Adapter
+- [ ] Implement the rich_text component adapter described in RFC section 3.3.
+Context:
+- RFC defines rich text as array of typed nodes:
+  - text: { type: "text", value: string, bold?: boolean, italic?: boolean, color?: string }
+  - link: { type: "link", text: string, to: string, trackEvent?: string }
+  - icon: { type: "icon", name: string, color?: string, size?: number }
+  - linebreak: { type: "linebreak" }
+- Types already defined: SDUIRichText, SDUITextNode, SDUILinkNode, SDUIIconNode,
+  SDUILineBreakNode (in models/index.ts)
+- Should render inline — all nodes in a single wrapping Text component
+  (React Native supports nested Text for inline formatting)
+- Links should use DS Text with underline style (similar to LinkAdapter)
+- Icons use DS Icon component inline
+- Register as "rich_text" type in adapter registry
+- Add mock payload using rich_text to test inline formatting
+Deliverable: RichTextAdapter registered in SDUI registry + unit tests +
+Storybook demo showing mixed bold/link/icon inline text.
- [ ] Find all design system component used in our screens and map all of it to
the SDUI system, including stories.

You are a senior code reviewer simulating a pull request review. The task was:

**SP989887GV [P1]**: Review the RFC document (rfc-conteudo-dinamico.txt) for features that are not yet implemented and don't have tasks in progress.txt, then add new tasks.

**What was done:**
1. Compared RFC sections 3.1-3.5 against the current SDUI implementation
2. Identified 5 gaps:
   - JSON Logic evaluator (RFC 3.5 — types exist but no evaluateRule function)
   - Component visibility conditional (SDUIComponent.visible field ignored by ScreenRenderer)
   - Button enabled conditional (ButtonAdapter ignores enabled rule)
   - Input validation rules (SDUIValidation types exist but no execution)
   - Rich text adapter (SDUIRichText types exist but no adapter component)
3. Added 4 new tasks (SP_0006.0 through SP_0006.3) to progress.txt as Phase 6

**The RFC defines these features (section 3.5 and 3.3):**
- JSON Logic: var, ==, !=, >, <, >=, <=, and, or, ! operators
- Conditionals: `enabled: { rule: {...} }` on buttons, `visible` on any component
- Validation: `validation: { rules: [{ rule, message }] }` on inputs
- Rich text: `{ type: "rich_text", children: [text/link/icon/linebreak nodes] }`

**What was already implemented (confirmed):**
- Types for ALL of the above (models/index.ts)
- Interpolation ({{var}} replacement) — DONE with 37 tests
- Registry + 11 adapters — DONE
- ScreenRenderer — DONE but doesn't check visibility conditionals

Please review:
1. Are the 4 new tasks complete and well-defined? Do they cover ALL RFC gaps?
2. Are there any other RFC features that might be missing from the analysis?
3. Are the task dependencies correct (SP_0006.1 and SP_0006.2 depend on SP_0006.0)?
4. Any concerns about the task scoping or approach?

Read the file at /Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/.claude/progress.txt (lines 666-750 approximately) and /Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/.claude/rfc-conteudo-dinamico.txt to verify.

Now let me do a comprehensive review. I'll compile my findings: ## Comprehensive PR Review: RFC Gap Analysis & Task Scoping ### 1. **Task Completeness & Correctness** I've verified all four newly added tasks (SP_0006.0–SP_0006.3) against the RFC and current codebase. Here are my findings: #### **SP_0006.0 — JSON Logic Evaluator** ✅ **WELL-DEFINED** - **RFC basis**: Section 3.5, lines 147–186 - **Operators specified**: `var`, `==`, `!=`, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`, `and`, `or`, `!` - **Types exist**: All defined in `src/services/sdui/models/index.ts` (SDUIRule, SDUIComparisonRule, SDUILogicalRule, SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional) - **Implementation strategy**: Create `evaluateRule(rule: SDUIRule, context: SDUIContext): boolean` - **Reusable context path resolution**: Can leverage existing `resolveContextPath()` from `interpolation.ts` - **Edge case handling**: "Invalid rules return false" (RFC 6.4) is correctly noted - **Test coverage requirement**: Comprehensive unit tests covering all operators + edge cases — appropriate given interpolation has 37 tests **Critical detail**: The task correctly identifies this must be a **pure function** with no side effects (important for declarative evaluation). --- #### **SP_0006.1 — Component Visibility & Button Enabled Conditionals** ✅ **WELL-DEFINED** - **RFC basis**: Section 3.5 lines 154–162 (button enabled), plus component `visible` pattern - **Current gaps confirmed**: - `SDUIComponent.visible?: SDUIConditional` exists in types but **ScreenRenderer ignores it entirely** (lines 41–66 of ScreenRenderer.tsx have zero checks for visibility) - `ButtonAdapter` accepts `disabled` prop but doesn't process `enabled: { rule }` from props (lines 15–35 of ButtonAdapter.tsx) - **Dependency on SP_0006.0**: Correct — needs `evaluateRule()` to compute boolean state - **Test scope**: Appropriately scoped to update `ScreenRenderer.test.tsx` and `adapters.test.tsx` plus a Storybook demo - **Implementation detail**: ScreenRenderer must conditionally render components; buttons must evaluate rules before rendering **Potential improvement**: The task should clarify whether `visible: false` should render a placeholder (for a11y/testing) or skip DOM entirely. In React Native, skipping is standard, so current scoping is sound. --- #### **SP_0006.2 — Input Validation Rules** ✅ **WELL-DEFINED** - **RFC basis**: Section 3.5 lines 171–186 - **Validation pattern**: `validation: { rules: [{ rule, message }] }` - **Current state**: Types exist (`SDUIValidationRule`, `SDUIValidation` in models) but **PointsInputAdapter completely ignores the validation prop** (lines 15–57 show no validation logic) - **Deliverable**: `validateField(value, validation: SDUIValidation, context): string | null` — correctly returns **first** failing rule's message (not all messages, which is important for UX) - **Dependency**: Correct — depends on SP_0006.0's `evaluateRule()` - **Integration scope**: Correctly identifies PointsInputAdapter's `textError` prop is the target; integration with error display is sound **Detail well-captured**: The task recognizes validation needs context access (for rules like `value > context.minAmount`), which is why context is a parameter. --- #### **SP_0006.3 — Rich Text Adapter** ✅ **WELL-DEFINED** - **RFC basis**: Section 3.3 lines 97–125 - **Node types defined**: All four node types specified in RFC and types exist: - `text: { type: "text", value, bold?, italic?, color? }` - `link: { type: "link", text, to, trackEvent? }` - `icon: { type: "icon", name, color?, size? }` - `linebreak: { type: "br" }` - **Current gap**: **NOT registered in adapter registry** (`adapters/index.ts` has 11 adapters; `rich_text` is missing) - **Implementation pattern**: Render inline in a single Text component (React Native supports nested Text) — **correct understanding** - **Design system alignment**: Links should use DS Text + underline, icons use DS Icon — correctly scoped to design system usage **Critical observation**: The task correctly notes that "rich_text" is in `SDUI_KNOWN_TYPES` (models/index.ts line 176) but **not in the adapter registry**, creating a silent gap that would trigger FallbackComponent rendering. --- ### 2. **RFC Features — Completeness Check** Let me verify no RFC sections were missed: | RFC Section | Feature | Status | Task | |---|---|---|---| | 3.1 | Screen structure (id, context, header, body, footer) | ✅ Implemented | — | | 3.2 | Component registry & rendering | ✅ Implemented (11 adapters) | — | | 3.3 | Rich text (text, link, icon, br nodes) | ❌ Types exist, no adapter | SP_0006.3 | | 3.4 | Context & interpolation ({{var}}) | ✅ Implemented (37 tests) | — | | 3.5 | JSON Logic operators | ❌ Types exist, no evaluator | SP_0006.0 | | 3.5 | Visibility conditionals | ❌ Type exists, not wired | SP_0006.1 | | 3.5 | Button enabled conditionals | ❌ Type exists, not wired | SP_0006.1 | | 3.5 | Input validation rules | ❌ Types exist, not wired | SP_0006.2 | | 6.1 | "What needs to be done" checklist | ✅ 3 of 4 done | — | **No missing features identified.** The 4 tasks cover all gaps. --- ### 3. **Task Dependencies — Correctness** ``` SP_0006.0 (JSON Logic evaluator) [INDEPENDENT] ↓ SP_0006.1 (visibility + enabled conditionals) [DEPENDS ON 0.0] SP_0006.2 (validation rules) [DEPENDS ON 0.0] SP_0006.3 (rich text adapter) [INDEPENDENT OF OTHERS] ``` **Dependency analysis**: - ✅ SP_0006.1 correctly depends on SP_0006.0 (needs `evaluateRule()`) - ✅ SP_0006.2 correctly depends on SP_0006.0 (needs `evaluateRule()`) - ✅ SP_0006.3 is independent (no conditional logic, pure rendering) - ✅ SP_0006.0 has no dependencies (uses existing `resolveContextPath`) **Critical observation**: The RFC call chain shows `evaluateRule` will be used by both conditional visibility/enabled AND validation, making it the critical path. Correct scoping. --- ### 4. **Scoping & Approach Concerns** #### **No Concerns** — The scoping is exemplary: 1. **Each task is atomic**: One feature per task, testable independently (except dependency chain) 2. **Test strategy is sound**: - SP_0006.0: Unit tests for all operators (following interpolation's 37-test pattern) - SP_0006.1: Renderer + adapter tests + Storybook visual - SP_0006.2: Validator tests + adapter integration + Storybook visual - SP_0006.3: Adapter tests + Storybook visual 3. **Deliverables are clear**: File paths, function signatures, integration points specified 4. **Storybook demos**: Appropriately requested for SP_0006.1–SP_0006.3 (visual/interactive features); not needed for SP_0006.0 (pure logic) --- ### 5. **Potential Enhancements (Optional Recommendations)** These are minor polish items, not deficiencies: 1. **SP_0006.0**: Add a note about performance implications if rules are deeply nested (JSON Logic doesn't guarantee linear complexity) — though for the RFC scope (simple comparisons), this is moot. 2. **SP_0006.1**: Consider adding an edge case test: what if `visible: { rule: malformed }` is passed? Should default to `true` (render) or `false` (hide)? Current task says "invalid rules return false" — this should clarify behavior for visibility specifically (hiding by default is safer UX). 3. **SP_0006.2**: The task mentions "integrate with PointsInputAdapter's error display" — should clarify whether validation occurs on blur, onChange, or on form submission. Current PointsInputAdapter has no form context, so this may belong in a parent component. Task scoping is correct (just the utility function), but implementation may need coordination with form-level handler. 4. **SP_0006.3**: Note that React Native Text component requires all children to be Text or string — linebreak should render as `\n` (newline character, not an actual `
` element). Task is implicitly correct, but could be explicit. --- ### **Final Verdict** **Grade: A-** The four tasks are **complete, well-defined, and correctly scoped**. They cover all RFC gaps for conditional logic and rich text rendering. Dependencies are accurate. The approach is methodical and testable. **Minor suggestions**: - SP_0006.1: Clarify default behavior for invalid visibility rules (hide or show) - SP_0006.2: Note form context boundary — validation logic is task scope, form handling is not - SP_0006.3: Clarify linebreak rendering as `\n` in Text, not DOM br **Recommendation**: All four tasks are ready for implementation. Start with SP_0006.0 (blocker for 0.1 and 0.2), then parallelize the remaining three.

# SP_0006.0 — JSON Logic Evaluator
- [ ] Implement the JSON Logic rule evaluator described in RFC section 3.5.

Context:
- RFC defines operators: var (data access), ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparison),
  and, or, ! (logical)
- Types already exist: SDUIRule, SDUIComparisonRule, SDUILogicalRule,
  SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional (in src/services/sdui/models/index.ts)
- Needs a function: evaluateRule(rule: SDUIRule, context: SDUIContext): boolean
- var operator uses resolveContextPath from interpolation.ts
- Invalid/malformed rules should return false (RFC 6.4: "Regras inválidas
  retornam false")
- Must be pure function, no side effects
- Reference: https://jsonlogic.com/

Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts with evaluateRule() +
comprehensive unit tests covering all operators and edge cases.

# SP_0006.0 — JSON Logic Evaluator
- [ ] Implement the JSON Logic rule evaluator described in RFC section 3.5.

Context:
- RFC defines operators: var (data access), ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparison),
  and, or, ! (logical)
- Types already exist: SDUIRule, SDUIComparisonRule, SDUILogicalRule,
  SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional (in src/services/sdui/models/index.ts)
- Needs a function: evaluateRule(rule: SDUIRule, context: SDUIContext): boolean
- var operator uses resolveContextPath from interpolation.ts
- Invalid/malformed rules should return false (RFC 6.4: "Regras inválidas
  retornam false")
- For `visible` conditionals specifically: invalid rules should default to
  true (show component) — hiding content by default on bad data is worse UX
- Must be pure function, no side effects
- Reference: https://jsonlogic.com/

Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts with evaluateRule() +
comprehensive unit tests covering all operators and edge cases.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [ ] Wire the JSON Logic evaluator into ScreenRenderer for component
      visibility and button enabled state.

  evaluateRule returns false

  disable button when rule evaluates to false
- Depends on: SP_0006.0 (evaluateRule)
- Update existing ScreenRenderer.test.tsx and adapters.test.tsx
- Add Storybook demo showing conditional visibility toggle

Deliverable: Components with `visible` conditionals are hidden/shown based on
context. Buttons with `enabled` rules are disabled/enabled dynamically.

# SP_0006.0 — JSON Logic Evaluator
- [ ] Implement the JSON Logic rule evaluator described in RFC section 3.5.

Context:
- RFC defines operators: var (data access), ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparison),
  and, or, ! (logical)
- Types already exist: SDUIRule, SDUIComparisonRule, SDUILogicalRule,
  SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional (in src/services/sdui/models/index.ts)
- Needs a function: evaluateRule(rule: SDUIRule, context: SDUIContext): boolean
- var operator uses resolveContextPath from interpolation.ts
- Invalid/malformed rules should return false (RFC 6.4: "Regras inválidas
  retornam false")
- For `visible` conditionals specifically: invalid rules should default to
  true (show component) — hiding content by default on bad data is worse UX
- Must be pure function, no side effects
- Reference: https://jsonlogic.com/

Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts with evaluateRule() +
comprehensive unit tests covering all operators and edge cases.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0006.1 — Component Visibility & Button Enabled Conditionals
- [ ] Wire the JSON Logic evaluator into ScreenRenderer for component
      visibility and button enabled state.

Context:
- SDUIComponent already has optional `visible: SDUIConditional` field
- ScreenRenderer currently ignores `visible` — should skip rendering when
  evaluateRule returns false
- ButtonAdapter should process `enabled: { rule: SDUIRule }` in props —
  disable button when rule evaluates to false
- Depends on: SP_0006.0 (evaluateRule)
- Update existing ScreenRenderer.test.tsx and adapters.test.tsx
- Add Storybook demo showing conditional visibility toggle

Deliverable: Components with `visible` conditionals are hidden/shown based on
context. Buttons with `enabled` rules are disabled/enabled dynamically.

  { "validation": { "rules": [{ "rule": {...}, "message": "error text" }] } }
- Types exist: SDUIValidationRule, SDUIValidation

  (returns first failing rule's message, or null if valid)
- Depends on: SP_0006.0 (evaluateRule)

- Scope: validation utility function only. Form-level orchestration (when to

+# SP_0006.0 — JSON Logic Evaluator
+- [ ] Implement the JSON Logic rule evaluator described in RFC section 3.5.
+
+Context:
+- RFC defines operators: var (data access), ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparison),
+  and, or, ! (logical)
+- Types already exist: SDUIRule, SDUIComparisonRule, SDUILogicalRule,
+  SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional (in src/services/sdui/models/index.ts)

+- var operator uses resolveContextPath from interpolation.ts
+- Invalid/malformed rules should return false (RFC 6.4: "Regras inválidas
+  retornam false")
+- For `visible` conditionals specifically: invalid rules should default to
+  true (show component) — hiding content by default on bad data is worse UX
+- Must be pure function, no side effects
+- Reference: https://jsonlogic.com/
+
+Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts with evaluateRule() +
+comprehensive unit tests covering all operators and edge cases.

+
+Context:

+  { "validation": { "rules": [{ "rule": {...}, "message": "error text" }] } }
+- Types exist: SDUIValidationRule, SDUIValidation
+- PointsInputAdapter has textError prop but no validation integration
+- Needs: validateField(value, validation: SDUIValidation, context): string | null

+- Depends on: SP_0006.0 (evaluateRule)
+- Should integrate with PointsInputAdapter's error display
+- Scope: validation utility function only. Form-level orchestration (when to

+
+Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/validation.ts with validateField() +

+

+

+
+Context:
+- RFC defines rich text as array of typed nodes:
+  - text: { type: "text", value: string, bold?: boolean, italic?: boolean, color?: string }
